method: vanilla literal refs + curated evidence-cited indexed arrays + known_RAM_map sized spans; custom = patch-only refs and wram.asm labels at vanilla-unreferenced addresses. Gaps are UNVETTED, never 'free'.
caveats: Literal refs from data-heavy banks can be data-disassembled-as-code artifacts (e.g. 'ld sp,$d38c' in bank_019 gfx). The tool keeps them: it must over-claim usage, never under-claim.
call/jp/jr operands into WRAM are classed 'suspect' and excluded from coverage (data-as-code; this ROM runs no code from WRAM).

Curated indexed arrays are the only positive free-space evidence source; a gap here still needs per-candidate vetting (pointer-walk loops, SVBK bank-2 windows in bank_051/052) before anything is placed in it.

indexed_arrays.name: party/storage monster array
indexed_arrays.evidence: GetMonsterDataPtr (bank_000: HL=HL+(A&$7F)*$95); label14_40b4 field writes; slots hold party+farm+eggs under one 20-slot limit (user-confirmed S54)

indexed_arrays.name: monster staging pseudo-slots $14/$15 (S56)
indexed_arrays.evidence: GetMonsterDataPtr masks $7F so indices $14/$15 reach $D665/$D6FA: breeding parents copied+deleted here (bank $0a flows; $16:SaveBrd_41ff reads fields at +$0BA4/+$0BA4+$95), link-trade transit ($15:jr_015_5aa5 send, $18 receive), bank $15 menu scratch ($CAC0:=$14/$15 writers). See MONSTER_DATA CF1 section + extracted/monster_walkers.json

indexed_arrays.name: library seen-bits
indexed_arrays.evidence: bank_007 jr_007_4251/PushAndReadParty loops: TestBitInArray($CA94, b) for b in 0..$EF -> 240 bits = 30 bytes $CA94-$CAB1

indexed_arrays.name: OAM shadow (DMA source page)
indexed_arrays.evidence: standard $FF46 DMA source page; wram.asm reserves $C000-$C09F before first label at $C0A0

indexed_arrays.name: screen staging block (tile-id shadow + pair)
indexed_arrays.evidence: S55: bank_006 bulk copy $C500->$C300 length $0200; save routine copies $C300 x$200 -> SRAM $BCC8 as a unit (bank_000 SavePartyData). Old 256-B extent was too small.

indexed_arrays.name: attr decompression staging
indexed_arrays.evidence: S55: every attr stream declares declen 256 to $C200 (bank_017 'ld hl,$c200 / call WaitLCDTransfer' x2 + banks $00/$06/$07/$0B; stream header = 2-byte declen); save routine copies $C200 x$100 -> SRAM $BEC8.

indexed_arrays.name: battle enemy tile buffer / staging source
indexed_arrays.evidence: known_RAM_map $C500 $240 (LoadBtl_7627, S52 HW); also the source of the bank_006 $0200 copy to $C300 (S55).

indexed_arrays.name: audio channel state + engine scalars
indexed_arrays.evidence: S55: ClearAudioChannels (bank_000 ~10701) inits 6 channels x 26 B from $DD80 (b=$06, stride $19+1); audio scalars enumerate to $DE2B. Supersedes the known_RAM_map 'INFERRED battle struct array' label for this range.

indexed_arrays.name: battle stat tables
indexed_arrays.evidence: known_RAM_map / patches wram.asm: 9 tables x 16 B ($DBA3-$DC32), HW-confirmed S52.

indexed_arrays.name: per-screen feature list
indexed_arrays.evidence: GATE_GENERATION.md §9 ($D793 entries [type,?,x,y]), bounded above by documented $D7B8

freed_windows.evidence: CF3 (S60): farm slots 3-19 are SRAM-resident at $A1FB+s*$95 (GetMonsterDataPtr fork, bank $73 entry 3; 48 walker advances hop the boundary; trade-recv + new-game clear redirected) — MONSTER_DATA 'CF3 as built (S60)'. TRANSIENT: the window's save image ($A3BA-$AD9E) is live farm storage, so CF3CopyToSRAM/CF3CopyFromSRAM (bank $73 entries 5/6) skip it in both directions — nothing here survives save+reload.

extended_index_findings.note: SetBitInArray($CA94, species) fires on add-to-storage (label14_40b4). Bits for new species land inside the vanilla-scanned extent, so no memory collision, but they COUNT toward the library completion counters (cp $64 / 100-monster reward checks in bank_007).
extended_index_findings.verdict: benign placement (inside scanned extent); affects library completion counts
